prerequisites: A Tremendous account. Sign up for a Sandbox account to test, and request Production access for live rewards.
An API key generated from the Tremendous dashboard under Team Settings > Developers. Keys are environment-specific, so generate a Sandbox key for testing and a separate Production key for live use.
Decide which environment to connect to (sandbox or production), since each uses a different API host and a different API key.

authMethods.name: API key (Bearer token)
authMethods.summary: Provide your Tremendous API key in the api_key field. The connector authenticates by sending it as an HTTP bearer token (Authorization: Bearer <api_key>). Generate the key in the Tremendous dashboard under Team Settings > Developers, and save it immediately because Tremendous treats keys as passwords and will not show the full key again. Use the matching key for the environment you select.

setupSteps.title: Create an API key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Tremendous dashboard and go to Team Settings > Developers. Generate an API key and copy it immediately, since Tremendous will not display the full key again after creation. Generate the key in the same environment you intend to connect to: a Sandbox account yields a Sandbox key, while a Production key requires Production access.

setupSteps.title: Set the environment
setupSteps.body: Set the environment config field to match your API key. The Sandbox environment uses the host testflight.tremendous.com and is for testing, while the Production environment uses the host api.tremendous.com for live data. Selecting the correct environment ensures requests go to the right host with the matching key.

setupSteps.title: Provide credentials and test
setupSteps.body: Enter your API key in the api_key field and confirm the environment value. The connector will send the key as a bearer token in the Authorization header to the selected host. Verify the connection succeeds before running a full sync.
